text: This video demonstrates the steps in the performance of laparoscopic duodenal jejunostomy for superior mesenteric artery syndrome.

Two stay sutures of 3-0 Vicryl are placed to align the proximal jejunum parallel to the duodenum, securing the position of these structures.

The surgeon creates enterotomies in both the proximal jejunum and the duodenum, preparing the bowel segments for anastomosis.

The surgeon creates a side-to-side duodenal jejunostomy using a 45mm linear stapler with a white load cartridge, joining the duodenum and proximal jejunum.

The surgeon inspects the interior of the duodenal jejunostomy anastomosis for any signs of bleeding, ensuring hemostasis within the newly formed connection between the bowel segments.

The surgeon closes the enterotomy with a running suture of Tuo polyester, securing the previously aligned bowel segments.

The surgeon closes the mesenteric defect using several interrupted polyester sutures, ensuring the defect is securely sealed.
